utterances.speaker: Maya
utterances.text: Welcome everyone. Let's go over where we are on the Rahid build before the demo tonight.

utterances.speaker: Alex
utterances.text: Sounds good. I'll keep notes as we go.

utterances.speaker: Maya
utterances.text: Our total budget is 5000 pounds. We already spent 500 on hosting and expect around 1000 for AI APIs.

utterances.speaker: Alex
utterances.text: So roughly three and a half thousand left to play with. That includes margin for surprise costs.

utterances.speaker: Maya
utterances.text: We should use Google Meet because live media access is the clearest demo story.

utterances.speaker: Alex
utterances.text: Agreed. The LiveKit room stays as our fallback if the Meet bot fights us.

utterances.speaker: Maya
utterances.text: The agent should listen to the meeting and create diagrams on the shared workspace.

utterances.speaker: Alex
utterances.text: Right, and the diagram surface lives on the web page, not inside Meet itself.

utterances.speaker: Maya
utterances.text: Generated PNGs should be stored in object storage, not directly in the database.

utterances.speaker: Alex
utterances.text: I'll set up an S3-compatible bucket and we'll just store the URL in SQLite.

utterances.speaker: Maya
utterances.text: Superlinked should power semantic retrieval and reranking over the transcript and prep docs.

utterances.speaker: Alex
utterances.text: Yeah, that's the wedge. Keyword search alone won't find paraphrased questions.

utterances.speaker: Maya
utterances.text: P3 retrieves context from P2, reasons with Gemini, and returns either an answer or Mermaid diagram code.

utterances.speaker: Alex
utterances.text: And the wake word is hey rahid, fuzzy match, case-insensitive.

utterances.speaker: Maya
utterances.text: P4 renders all visuals on the web workspace and polls the events feed.

utterances.speaker: Alex
utterances.text: I'll also wire the post-meeting Q&A box on the same page so we don't ship two apps.

utterances.speaker: Maya
utterances.text: Alex will wire P3 to the retrieval endpoint before the final demo.

utterances.speaker: Alex
utterances.text: Confirmed. I'll do that right after lunch and run a paraphrased query through it.

utterances.speaker: Maya
utterances.text: Last thing: Aikido scans the repo at the end, so no secrets ever land in commits.

utterances.speaker: Alex
utterances.text: Env vars only, .env is gitignored, and we ship a .env.example. We're good.
